source.title: Documented seed: bottleneck shift page
source.source_note: This extraction is reconstructed from the documented OpenClaw style lessons and validated slide patterns, not from the original slide screenshot.

claim: The delivery bottleneck is shifting from generation speed to controlled execution and review quality.
visual_anchor: A dominant left-side thesis block with a bottom visual object grounds the page.

composition.structure: Large thesis zone on the left, stacked support cards on the right, grounded by a lower visual panel.
composition.alignment_logic: Left and right columns share the same top and bottom bounds; support cards align to one right-side grid.
composition.asymmetry: The page is not symmetric, but both columns live inside one shared content frame.
composition.image_usage: A supporting image or abstract visual in the lower left adds weight and prevents an empty lower half.
highlight_grammar: Oversized thesis statement with selective accent words
small signal labels on support cards
gradient or glow confined to the lower visual panel

components.position_note: Three aligned cards on the right

why_it_works: One sharp thesis drives the page instead of diffusing attention across equal cards.
The lower visual object fixes the common top-heavy dark-theme failure mode.
The support cards provide evidence without weakening the drama of the page.
anti_patterns: Leaving a large empty lower container makes the page look unfinished.
Misaligning left and right columns breaks executive polish even when asymmetry is intended.
Shrinking the thesis until the page reads like a normal content slide removes the strategic feel.

do_not_copy_blindly: The exact sentence shape of the thesis
adaptation_notes: Use this pattern only when the page needs one strong directional statement.
Always design the lower-left area as an actual object, not an empty container.
